reviews.text: I went to see a doctor at the Medical chambers. The doctor was great in person and diagnosed the issue very quickly. However, the treatment did not work and trying to get replies from the doctor over email was very very difficult with slow response times (about a week). It was not ideal when you have a patient concerned and needed to know how to proceed. All in all the appointment was very expensive for such poor post treatment care!

I do not recommend Dr. Bataille or The Medical Chambers Kensington. The skin check was shockingly superficial, and the interaction dismissive. I found a serious lack of ethical care, especially around informed consent and communication. Procedures were done without clear explanation or discussion of options, and I was not involved in decisions about my treatment. The billing was confusing and non-transparent, with unexpected double charges (one for the doctor, one for the clinic - both, overpriced). When I raised concerns, the response was slow, dismissive and disrespectful towards the patient, including repeatedly mischaracterising reasons for concern. This isn’t about my diagnosis or outcome—it’s about respecting patients’ rights to clear, honest information and involvement in their care. Unacceptable.

Very disappointed in the service I received at Dr Kuchai's clinic. Dr Kuchai herself was great, but the admin/finance department were all over the place. First I was invoiced hundreds of pounds extra for tests I never had, then I was also invoiced £90 for a phone call that never even happened. Finally, when I asked to renew my prescription, I was prescribed the wrong medication. I notified them of this about a week ago and there has been no update. Effectively - every step post-appointment was a blunder.

December 2024. Be very careful: after being a client for 10 years, I learnt last week that some invoices, dated from NINE months ago, hadn't been covered by my insurance company (not sure why, but maybe). Medical Chambers just decided to charge my card, without giving me even a month delay (when I waited 9 months to know that there was an issue). This is just a business, definitely not "client centric" as they claim to be.
UPDATE Feb 2025: they now claim that the mistake is mine because they found an email dated 2023 (!!) in which I made a mistake regarding my insurance company. But this email was for one specific appointment, for which they managed to process my insurance details without any issues :-)
And I double check my insurance details every single time I walk in, with their reception staff ... anyway, they obviously won't take responsibility for anything: you, as clients, will always be in the wrong.

I have visited this place in order to see a qualified GP. I was more than happy to go private as I hoped I would save time and get better more personalised care.
In reality this experience was quite awful.
As their GP diagnosed that I don't have an ear infection (the problem that made me want to see a GP in the first place), but instead I may have a very serious neurological disorder and sent me to an emergency room that same evening.
After waiting for hours in an emergency section of the hospital and experiencing a lot of anxiety about the possibility that she presented me with I saw the ENT specialist. As soon as the specialist looked into my ear she confirmed that I have an ear infection and should be treated with antibiotics.
Why did I go private? Spend money? Get misdiagnosed, scared and still end up in a public hospital where I got much better care?
My advice.. AVOID THIS PLACE

Posting on behalf of my daughter but this surely has one of the most abysmal customer experiences for an expensive London medical chambers I’ve ever experienced in my time looking after her. Poor admin meant we had issues with our insurance …

I would give zero stars to Flammarion. My child diagnosed with allergy and at her report she says "we will consider an epipen in the future" she was that vague, didn't raise a concern and let us believe that we can battle an accident just with citirizine, which was totally unprofessional the least and it could lead to serious issues, if you consider that from NHS gp was told to carry 2epipens at all times. It took several emails from us requesting to send her report, she eventually sent it around 2 months later and she didn’t send it to our GP who could as a last resourse spot the omission and refer us for further investigations. Subsequently, we visited Cromwell, were prescribed on the spot with 2 epipens, and referred for blood tests. Our GP provided 2 more epipens to keep at my child's activities. Our Cromwell Dr. suggested OIT, which the sooner started, the greater the chances are for a successful outcome. She made us lose precious time. She didn't make us aware of the importance of having a fatal allergy.

Very bad experience with medical chambers. My husband was in a very critical condition because of an allergy and asked for an emergency appointment. They never returned his call and Dr GARDETTE never responded. It’s not a good medical centre and MEDICARE is much better. Just don’t go there
The short version: brilliant, kind and thorough consultant (Dr George); terrible reception/admin service. …

If you are not even honest about the small things, how can you expect others to believe you will be honest about the big things? Let me tell you about my experience:

They have a policy where they charge you £2 for a mask, which at the beginning of the crisis at least they did *not* notify patients (or, at least, me) about when booking the appointment over the phone (maybe it was in their fine print)

I said at reception that I did not want to pay the fee. They offered to give me a mask for free, so I went ahead with the appointment. However, they went ahead and charged my card. I sent them an email which did not get replied to for three months. I received a reply saying that their sign outside the clinic clearly states that masks cost £2. I wrote back to the person re-explaining that I did not ask to purchase a mask but was offered one for free. However, she did not reply for a month. I called today and a lady with an uncooperative attitude picked up the phone, first saying to me her manager was on another line, then saying she was not willing to speak to me and asking me to send another email. When I stated that I had sent an email already, they said to me they were not willing to speak to me any further and said they were sorry.

I had an appointment with Dr Bataille 2 years ago following what seemed like an enduring skin allergy.
I spent a total of £775 (£195 initial appointment+£460 test and sample taking+ …

My ill partner paid 300 quid blood test they lost in transit. Took them 3 days to figure that out. So much money and their lab is not even working over the weekend?! Obviously took no responsibility, for 300 pounds...

I can't believe i forgot to leave a review here.
Over a year ago I was at work and my wife and son came to see Dr Anne Buk-Serero. My son was ill, he was 8 months old. Dr Anne Buk-Serero came inside the waiting room to part treat him, embarassing my wife when she pulled the pampers in a hurry my son started crying, everybody was looking at them, we made a complaint and there was no follow-up and no refund.
How did i remembered to leave a review? My son is ill again and i'm looking for a private clinic but i am definetly NOT choosing this one.

I am very disappointed with the service, I was told that I could bring my blood test results and scans And diagnosis from my Nhs gp to my consultation here and would be advised on a treatment plan. Instead you want me to repeat the same blood tests And scans I’ve done to take more money from me!

I was for a gynecology visit, I'm disappointed because I didn't get a solution for what I went for, you just pay and get nothing.  No one asks you what you are for, but they just add a list of things to do and in the end, I got nothing.
Dental Practice - Dr. F.  - Extremely disappointing, no patience nor empathy for kids. Unable to explain and appease young patient (or parents)
Had an appointment cancelled and was not informed until the moment I arrived

First of all, I highly doubt their positive reviews. They're not transparent about their test prices; it's the worst medical center. I consulted the handocrinologist for my son regarding his thyroid, Kevin Shottliff. He ordered blood tests, …

I explicitly told the staff not to use a specific credit card for my payment, and they acknowledged this instruction twice. Despite that, they used the card anyway.

This mistake isn’t just inconvenient—it could have caused serious issues, such as overdrafts or problems with company accounts. Acknowledging instructions but ignoring them feels negligent and unprofessional. It really was quite a headache for me and something that was easily avoidable.

I hope the practice takes better care in handling billing in the future, as attention to detail in these matters is critical.

I have been registered with this practice for about a year and have never had an on time appointment.
On four previous occasions I've had to wait between 20 and 45 minutes to be seen. Pretty shoddy for a private medical practice. The doctors are good but the time keeping is just horrendous.

Nothing to say about the doctor that I saw today. She was excellent. But I'm angry about the bad staff at the reception (which ruined at the end my visit) Where i was literally accused of wanting fraud because I just asked to separates the …

I would give 0 stars to the reception staff if I could. We were held at the reception for too long with a crying toddler on our hands. They couldn't believe we hadn't received a confirmation letter and a registration form by email though I had called them two days in a row and they couldn't fix that.
As for the doctor paeditrician we expected a more thorough examination.
